codeReview.summary: The original failure is not adequately addressed. Commit `1b9551f` changes only `src/api/reviewApi.test.ts` and `src/components/review/FileUpload.test.ts`, while the feature contract required direct fixes to the persistence and returned-review timeout baseline area (`useReviewStore.persistence.test.ts`, `useUserStore.persistence.test.ts`, `ResubmissionTaskList.test.ts`, `WorkflowReturnDialog.test.ts`, or their real dependencies) and elimination of the unexpected `localhost:3000` access driving those failures. The current workspace still contains no scoped implementation follow-up in the required files or dependencies.

issues.description: `1b9551f` relaxes `reviewApi` URL assertions to accept either relative paths or `http://localhost:3100`, but this file is outside the feature's required persistence/review-timeout scope. The transcript, handoff, and commit diff show no direct change to `src/composables/useReviewStore.persistence.test.ts`, `src/composables/useUserStore.persistence.test.ts`, `src/components/review/ResubmissionTaskList.test.ts`, `src/components/review/WorkflowReturnDialog.test.ts`, or any implementation dependency they exercise, so the feature still does not implement the requested baseline repair.

The second changed file rewrites drag/drop upload assertions in `FileUpload.test.ts`, which the feature description explicitly called out as insufficient on its own. This may reduce unrelated suite flakiness, but it does not demonstrate that the returned-task persistence and review UI timeout regressions were fixed at their source, and the handoff still acknowledges residual `localhost:3000` warnings during full-suite execution.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skipped the skill's required TDD/manual-verification flow and instead retargeted the task to unrelated tests after seeing noisy `localhost:3000` output. For narrowly scoped baseline-fix features, the current `designer-tracking-worker` procedure does not clearly force workers to prove that changed files overlap the feature's explicitly named failing surfaces before committing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Volumes/DPC/work/plant-code/plant3d-web/.factory/skills/designer-tracking-worker/SKILL.md requires investigating every affected surface and verifying changed behavior; transcript entry for worker session `bd13771d-e2d4-485f-9bb6-2c7d59e9ebb9` shows only `src/api/reviewApi.test.ts` and `src/components/review/FileUpload.test.ts` were edited and agent-browser verification was skipped.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/shared guidance does not document that baseline `npm test` may emit pre-existing `localhost:3000` warnings unrelated to the assigned returned-task persistence surfaces. That noise appears to have misdirected the fix toward `reviewApi` expectations instead of the explicitly scoped failing files.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The worker handoff says full `npm test` still logs refused `localhost:3000` warnings after the fix, while `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/ed11d871-9b91-4833-a106-380682e638ec/AGENTS.md` and `/Volumes/DPC/work/plant-code/plant3d-web/.factory/library/` do not record this baseline-noise caveat for the mission.

summary: Review failed again. The new commit `1b9551f` only adjusts `src/api/reviewApi.test.ts` and `src/components/review/FileUpload.test.ts`; it does not directly repair the named persistence/returned-review timeout baseline surfaces or prove that the unexpected `localhost:3000` access affecting those tests was removed from that scope. Current workspace inspection shows no newer relevant changes in the required files, so the milestone blocker remains unresolved.
